plan: Reduce SPY to 20% to lower concentration.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Increase QQQI and QQQ to 25% each for broad market and income stability.
Maintain a cash buffer by reducing overall exposure.
Monitor META for a future entry if conditions align with conservative strategy.
conservative.read: This mode targets a stable ~1.5%/month with capital preservation. It significantly de-risks the portfolio, reducing potential drawdowns. Returns will be modest, prioritizing safety over growth.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its downtrend and being below stop.
Reduce SPAL to 5% to manage leveraged ETF exposure.
Add XLK for diversified tech exposure and increase SMH and CRWD.
This balances broad market with quality tech growth and managed leverage.
balanced.read: This mode aims for ~2.5%/month growth with a balanced risk approach. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while still seeking growth opportunities. Expect moderate drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its downtrend and being below stop.
Increase SPAL to 10% and SMH to 20% for leveraged sector exposure.
Add CRWD to 15% and XLK to 10% for growth.
Buy MRVL at 10% for its high risk/reward setup.
aggressive.read: This mode targets ~4%/month growth by leaning into leverage and concentration. It carries significantly higher risk and potential for large drawdowns. Daily decay and rebalancing in leveraged ETFs can amplify losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Increase QQQI to 30% for enhanced income.
Add QQQ to 20% for broad market exposure.
Reduce CRWD to 5% to lower high-beta single name exposure.
income.read: This mode aims for ~1%/month from distributions, prioritizing yield over growth. It significantly reduces volatility and high-beta exposure. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline during market downturns.
